claims.text: This file is the quick operating guide for tech-stack-generator/.
- Separate Vite app inside the Oando repo
- Source of the interactive tech-stack docs site
On Windows PowerShell, use npm.cmd instead of npm if needed.
- npm run dev - start the docs site
- npm run build - typecheck and build to ../tech-stack-docs/
- npm run preview - preview the built site
- npm run typecheck - TypeScript check only
- npm run test:coverage - Vitest with V8 coverage
- npm run coverage - alias for coverage
Current package test coverage is intentionally small and checks the package wiring:
1. Read the package Vite config from disk.
3. Verify the tech stack data arrays are present and valid.
- src/data/ - navigation and tech stack data
- tests/ - Vitest tests for package behavior
- Keep the source package name tech-stack-generator
- If the test suite expands, update this walkthrough and the package README together

A standalone, modifiable documentation site showcasing the entire technology stack, architecture, workflows, and development processes of the **Oando Platform** (a furniture company platform at oando.co.in featuring a 2D/3D room planner, product catalog, CRM, and admin dashboard).

Built with **Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS**, this site documents the production stack (Next.js 16, Supabase, Fabric.js, Three.js/R3F, Zustand, Drizzle ORM, Playwright + Vitest, Vercel) in an interactive, searchable, responsive interface.
> **Windows / PowerShell:** use npm.cmd instead of npm (e.g. npm.cmd run dev) — this matches the conventions of the main Oando Platform repository.
| npm run dev | Start Vite dev server with HMR |
| npm run build | Type-check + production build to ../tech-stack-docs/ |
| npm run preview | Preview the production build locally |
| npm run typecheck | Run TypeScript compiler in check mode |
| npm run test | Run the Vitest package suite |
| npm run test:coverage | Run the Vitest suite with V8 coverage |
See [Readme_Techstack.md](./Readme_Techstack.md) for the package runbook, test flow, and coverage summary.
See [COVERAGE-REPORT.md](./COVERAGE-REPORT.md) for the current file-wise coverage breakdown.
1. **Overview** — Landing page with tech stack summary, key technologies, and navigation
2. **Tech Stack** — All 40+ dependencies grouped by category with versions and roles
3. **Architecture** — System diagrams (Mermaid), App Router structure, planner data flow, auth flow
4. **Features** — Deep-dives into Planner (Fabric.js + R3F), Catalog, CRM, Admin
5. **Code Organization** — Directory layout, module conventions, import patterns
6. **Database** — PostgreSQL schema (ER diagram), Drizzle ORM, migrations, RLS policies
7. **API Design** — Route table, route handler + Server Action examples, error conventions
8. **Testing** — Vitest + Playwright + coverage strategy with code examples
9. **Deployment** — Vercel CI/CD pipeline, release:gate steps, environment variables
10. **Security** — Auth, RLS, secrets management, rate limiting, secret scanning
11. **Performance** — Server components, image optimization, 3D performance, Web Vitals
12. **Workflows** — Git flow, daily dev loop, commit conventions, common task guides
- **Searchable** — Fuse.js fuzzy search across all documentation sections (sidebar search box)
- **Collapsible sections** — Long content folds into expandable panels
- **Syntax highlighting** — highlight.js for all code blocks with copy-to-clipboard
- **Mermaid diagrams** — Architecture, data flow, ER, sequence, and flowchart diagrams
- **Responsive sidebar** — Collapsible on mobile with a drawer toggle
- **Dark theme** — Modern dark UI built with Tailwind CSS
- **Category filtering** — Filter tech stack by category (Frontend, Canvas & 3D, Backend, etc.)
1. Create a new file in src/pages/ (e.g. ReleaseNotes.tsx)
3. Add a nav entry in src/data/navigation.ts:
4. If using a new icon, add it to the iconMap in src/components/Sidebar.tsx.
### Add a new technology to the stack
Edit src/data/techStack.ts and append a new TechItem object:
Edit src/hooks/useSearch.ts and add entries to the searchIndex array. Each entry needs an id, title, path, section, content (searchable text), and optional tags.

Edit tailwind.config.js to change the brand and accent color scales. The Mermaid diagram theme is configured in src/components/MermaidDiagram.tsx. The syntax highlighting theme is in src/index.css (.hljs-* classes).
- <CodeBlock code="..." language="typescript" title="example.ts" /> — Renders highlighted code with copy button
- <MermaidDiagram chart="flowchart TB\n A --> B" title="Title" /> — Renders a Mermaid diagram
## Tech Used to Build This Site
- **TypeScript 5** — Type safety (this docs site uses TS 5; the main platform uses TS 6.x)

This documentation site is a **separate Vite app** that lives inside the main Oando Platform repository at tech-stack-generator/ and builds to tech-stack-docs/. It has its own package.json and does not share dependencies with the main Next.js app. Keep it aligned with the authoritative repo docs in Readme.md, AGENTS.md, docs/architecture/CSS-SOLUTION.md, and plans/ so it mirrors the current stack rather than drifting into stale paths.
_Generated for the Oando Platform. See the main repository for the production codebase._

Every protected route starts with getUser() and returns 401 if no session. Defense in depth alongside RLS.
All request bodies and query params validated with Zod schemas before touching the database. Fail fast.
Next.js Server Actions used for mutations alongside route handlers. Both follow the same validation pattern.
Responses use a consistent { data, error } shape with TypeScript types matching the Supabase generated types.

Routes are illustrative of the patterns in
. Actual paths may vary — check the codebase for the live list.
All API responses follow a consistent shape. Errors include a message and optional details for validation errors.

How the Oando Platform is structured, from high-level system design to data flow and authentication.

The platform is a flat-root Next.js 16 application. The client bundle contains the React UI, Fabric.js 2D canvas, and React Three Fiber 3D viewer — all coordinated by Zustand stores. Server-side rendering and middleware run on Vercel's edge, proxying auth and data requests to Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage). Static catalog assets are served from Cloudflare R2.
The app uses Next.js 16's App Router with a flat-root structure. Route groups organize marketing vs. workspace without affecting URLs.
allow different layouts and middleware behavior without URL prefixes.
and is imported by app routes. This keeps route files thin and collocates feature logic, components, and tests.
The planner is the most complex feature. User actions on the Fabric canvas flow through a Zustand store, which syncs to the R3F 3D viewer and persists to Supabase with debouncing.

Supabase Auth with magic-link (OTP) email authentication. Session cookies are validated in Next.js middleware for protected routes. Row Level Security enforces user-scoped data access at the database level.
CSS is organized into a token-driven system with no hex colors in components. All colors come from

Root tsconfig.json owns path aliases. Common: @/lib/*, @/features/*, @/components/*, @/app/*
Use `import "server-only"` in server modules to prevent client bundle leaks. Supabase server client uses service role key.
Each feature owns its components, hooks, store, and tests. App routes import from features/, keeping route files thin.
All colors come from app/css/core/tokens/theme.css. Components reference CSS variables or Tailwind tokens, never raw hex.
Strict mode enabled. No implicit any, noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters. TS 6.x required.
Features expose a public API via index.ts. Internal modules use deep imports only when necessary.
How the codebase is structured — directory layout, module conventions, and import patterns.
A typical planner route imports feature code, which in turn pulls from shared lib utilities.

Apply pending migrations to linked Supabase
Generate TypeScript types from Supabase schema
PostgreSQL on Supabase — schema design, Drizzle ORM, migrations, and Row Level Security.
The database stores users (Supabase Auth), plans (planner save state), products (catalog), leads (CRM), and supporting tables for images, variants, and activity tracking.
Every table with user-owned data has RLS enabled. Policies filter rows based on the authenticated user's JWT claims — a user can only read/write their own plans, leads, and activity.
Drizzle provides type-safe schema definitions and a query builder that compiles to SQL. It runs alongside the Supabase client for direct DB access in scripts and server routes.
Migrations are applied via dedicated scripts that connect to the linked Supabase instance. Drizzle Kit generates migration SQL from schema changes.
Supabase generates TypeScript types from the live database schema into
. These power the typed Supabase client so queries are fully type-checked at compile time.

Deep-dive into each feature module of the Oando Platform.

The planner combines a Fabric.js 2D canvas for drawing rooms and placing furniture with a React Three Fiber 3D viewer that renders the same scene in real-time. The Fabric.js canvas replaced the previous tldraw implementation in the 2026-06-18 session for better control over furniture object model and snapping.
Main Fabric.js canvas instance. Initializes the canvas, handles object events, and bridges to the Zustand store.
Top-level workspace container that lays out the canvas, toolbar, library panel, and 3D viewer in resizable panels.
Tool selection bar — select, wall, draw room, place furniture. Sets the active tool in the store.
Searchable product library panel. Uses Fuse.js for fuzzy search across catalog items. Drag to canvas to place.
Right-click context menu for selected objects — rotate, duplicate, delete, bring to front.
The bridge layer converts Fabric.js objects into Three.js scene elements. Each furniture item has a GLTF model loaded from R2 CDN, positioned and rotated based on its 2D representation.
renders the Three.js scene declaratively with React Three Fiber. Uses drei for OrbitControls, Environment lighting, and GLTF model loading.
Furniture catalog with Supabase + R2 CDN

The catalog stores product metadata in Supabase PostgreSQL while images and 3D models live in Cloudflare R2. A CDN asset pipeline uploads, organizes, and audits assets. TanStack Query caches product lists client-side with automatic invalidation on mutations.

The CRM module manages customers, leads, and a sales pipeline. Recharts powers analytics dashboards showing conversion rates, lead sources, and revenue tracking. All data is scoped to the authenticated user via Supabase RLS.
The admin dashboard provides CRUD operations for the product catalog, user management, and platform-wide analytics. Access is restricted to admin-role users via RLS policies and middleware checks.
— featuring a 2D/3D room planner, product catalog, CRM, and admin dashboard built with modern web technology.
The Oando Platform is a flat-root
serving a furniture company's complete digital presence. The codebase uses a single monorepo structure with feature-based module organization under
and the Next.js App Router under
— a sophisticated 2D/3D floorplan designer powered by
(canvas drawing, furniture placement, wall tools) combined with
for real-time 3D preview. The planner integrates directly with the product catalog via Supabase.
with Cloudflare R2 CDN assets, a
, and a fully SEO-optimized marketing site.
Next.js 16 App Router uses Server Components by default. Data fetching happens on the server, reducing client JS.
Server-side Supabase queries reduce client waterfalls
Catalog images served from R2 CDN with next/image for responsive sizing and lazy loading.
TanStack Query for client cache + Next.js fetch caching for server data.
Code splitting and dynamic imports keep the initial bundle small.
Dynamic import for Fabric.js and Three.js
Lighthouse + @vercel/speed-insights track LCP, FID, CLS in production.
React Three Fiber scene optimized for smooth 60fps interaction.
LOD (level of detail) for distant objects
Strategies for fast load times, smooth 3D rendering, and efficient data fetching.
Fabric.js and Three.js are large libraries. They're dynamically imported so they only load on the planner route, keeping marketing pages lean.
3D furniture models are processed with gltf-transform before upload to R2: Draco compression, unused attribute removal, and texture resizing.

Prevents brute force on magic link requests

import — any accidental client import fails at build time.
